condition: Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia / Small Lymphocytic Lymphoma
procedure_name: CT for Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia (Fludarabine + Cyclophosphamide (FC))
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience/Fellowship in Hematology/Medical Oncology)
clinical_indications: Complete blood counts confirming diagnosis of CLL
Flow cytometry (in peripheral blood or bone marrow) confirming diagnosis of CLL
Lymph node biopsy confirming diagnosis of small lymphocytic lymphoma (SLL), treated like CLL
Clinical notes indicating presence or absence of B symptoms
Clinical notes indicating lymphadenopathy with size
Clinical notes indicating splenomegaly with size
Clinical notes indicating comorbidities and indication for therapy
Clinical notes indicating prior treatment for CLL (name of regimen, cycles, date of last cycle, response)
USG abdomen + pelvis or CECT chest + abdomen + pelvis with evidence of bulky lymph nodes
USG abdomen + pelvis or CECT chest + abdomen + pelvis with evidence of massive splenomegaly
USG abdomen + pelvis or CECT chest + abdomen + pelvis with evidence of other organ involvement
Previous 3 CBC reports showing doubling of lymphocyte counts in last 6 months

preauth.label: Flow cytometry (either in peripheral blood or bone marrow- confirming diagnosis of CLL)
Lymph node biopsy (confirming diagnosis of small lymphocytic lymphoma)

Clinical notes (Performance status, Presence or absence of B symptoms, lymphadenopathy with size, splenomegaly with size comorbidities and indication for therapy, Prior treatment for CLL (name of regimen, cycles, date of last cycle, response))
USG abdomen + pelvis or CECT chest + abdomen + Pelvis – with evidence of bulky lymph nodes, massive splenomegaly or other organ involvement
Previous 3 CBC reports showing doubling of lymphocyte counts in last 6 months
Details of prior treatment (optional) : Date of start and the end of therapy, name of the regimen and response to therapy

claim.label: Reports of the tests (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry etc. listed under preauthorization)
Discharge summary of day care department

Response assessment (details after every 4 cycles and at the end of planned therapy showing improvement in hemoglobin, platelet counts, lymphadenopathy, splenomegaly and symptoms history and clinical examination, documented on USG/CECT)

ppd_preauth.q: Is flow cytometry or biopsy report available?
Is the indication of therapy written and supported by documentation?

Is the patient aged >65 years, or has poor performance status (3/4), multiple comorbidities (poorly controlled diabetes), renal dysfunction, or autoimmune hemolytic anemia, and FC or FCR regimen is selected?
Is abnormal creatinine present and FC or FCR regimen selected?
Is poor performance status (3/4) present and FC or FCR regimen selected?
Is 17 p deletion present and FC or FCR regimen selected?
Has the patient received prior treatment with FC or FCR (permitted once in a lifetime) and this regimen is selected again?
Has the patient received prior treatment with the same regimen within one year, or without response to the same treatment earlier?

common_queries: Missing flow cytometry or biopsy report.
Indication for therapy is not written or supporting documents for indication are missing.
FC or FCR regimen selected for patient aged >65 years, poor performance status (3/4), multiple comorbidities, renal dysfunction, or autoimmune hemolytic anemia.
Abnormal creatinine detected, and FC or FCR regimen is selected.
Poor performance status (3/4) detected, and FC or FCR regimen is selected.
17 p deletion detected, and FC or FCR regimen is selected.
FC or FCR regimen is selected for a patient with prior treatment of the same regimen (once in a lifetime limit exceeded).
Same regimen is selected for prior treatment within one year, or if no response to same treatment earlier.
Missing bar code for drugs at claims submission.
Missing or incomplete test reports (pathology, radiology, etc.) at claims submission.
Missing discharge summary (inpatient or day care) at claims submission.
Missing charts of chemotherapy regimen or CBC before each cycle at claims submission.
Missing response assessment details after 4 cycles or at end of planned therapy.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Essential Medical Personnel: Qualified Medical Oncologist/ Hematologist; Essential Medical Infrastructure: Chemotherapy unit (Day care/Injection room/Ward)
clinical_key_pointers: FC and FCR can be used once in a lifetime as first or second line therapy.

Bendamustine + Rituximab, Rituximab + Chlorambucil, Rituximab + Lenalidomide can be used more than once as first or subsequent line therapy (same regimen can be repeated after 1 year if patient had response to earlier course).
Lenalidomide can be repeated more than once, in the 1st or 2nd line.
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy should be avoided in CLL.
contraindications: FC is contraindicated if age >65 years.
FC is contraindicated if poor performance status (3/4).
FC is contraindicated if multiple comorbidities (e.g., poorly controlled diabetes).
FC is contraindicated if renal dysfunction.
FC is contraindicated if autoimmune hemolytic anemia.
FC is contraindicated if 17 p deletion is present.
